sources.name: Google Developer Documentation Style Guide
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 1: Highly effective (1.0). Run 2 (DOC-3137): Zero explicit citations, effectiveness 0.5. Run 3 (DOC-2847): Effectiveness 0.67 — REC-001 (boolean documentation) and REC-005 (defaults) cited by T-001. REC-006 (action verb method descriptions) not cited. Weighted effectiveness across 3 runs: 0.56. Run 4 (DOC-3167): REC-005 (descriptive link text) cited by T-002, T-005, T-007. Other sub-sources (notices, tables, api) had 0 citations. Overall citation rate: 10% (1 of 10 approved recommendations). Weighted effectiveness across 4 runs: 0.45.

sources.name: Diátaxis Documentation Framework
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 1: Effective (0.71). Run 2 (DOC-3137): 0.25 explicit. Run 3 (DOC-2847): 0.0 — REC-007 and REC-008 not cited by any task. Structural guidance implicitly followed via INV-structure-* invariants. Framework value is in initial classification (reference vs how-to), not per-task execution. Weighted effectiveness across 3 runs: 0.32. Run 4 (DOC-3167): REC-004 (goal-oriented imperatives) not cited. Task was reference/gap-fill documentation, not how-to guides. Weighted effectiveness across 4 runs: 0.24.

sources.name: Microsoft Writing Style Guide
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 1: Highly effective (1.0) — step-by-step, reference, code examples all contributed. Run 2 (DOC-3137): Zero explicit citations. Code example patterns (REC-014) likely internalized. Step-by-step guidance less relevant for developer-focused API documentation run. Effectiveness adjusted to 0.5 reflecting mixed explicit/implicit use across 2 runs.

sources.name: Write the Docs — Documentation Guide
sources.assessmentDetail: Unused across 2 pipeline runs. Low priority for API reference and how-to documentation tasks. May become relevant for community-facing or contribution guide documentation.

sources.name: Kentico Xperience admin UI component reference — icon classes, interaction verbs, field behaviors
sources.rationale: Business-user pages (TASK-011, TASK-012, TASK-013) faced unique challenges around UI interaction vocabulary and icon accuracy that weren't covered by any research source. A Kentico-specific admin UI style reference would help.
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 2 (DOC-3137): T-012 (business-user page) passed first attempt without needing this source. Need is less acute but still relevant for future business-user-heavy runs. Keeping as pending-review with low priority.

sources.name: Web research — registration pattern documentation practices
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 2 (DOC-3137): REC-012 (registration pattern documentation) originated from this source but was internalized without explicit citation. T-001 correctly documented the ActivityEvaluationRegister pattern.

sources.name: Web research — lifecycle hook documentation patterns
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 2 (DOC-3137): REC-009 (explicit 'not called when' documentation) and REC-010 (pipeline flow with dual paths) both originated here and were internalized by T-001 without explicit citation. Valuable source for lifecycle/pipeline documentation.

sources.name: Microsoft .NET C# Coding Conventions
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 3 (DOC-2847): REC-012 (object initializers) cited by T-001, T-003, T-004. All parameter object construction uses object initializer syntax. Direct 1:1 recommendation→output match. Effectiveness: 1.0.

sources.name: Microsoft Named and Optional Arguments Guide
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 3 (DOC-2847): REC-003 (named parameters in 3+ param calls) cited by 5 tasks (T-001, T-003, T-004, T-007, T-009) with 4/5 first-attempt. REC-010 (named arguments for skipping optionals) cited by same 5 tasks. Top-performing source for this run. Effectiveness: 1.0.

sources.name: Microsoft Code Examples Style Guide
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 3 (DOC-2847): REC-009 (start simple, build complexity) cited by T-001. REC-011 (comment Kentico-specific methods) cited by T-001, T-003, T-004, T-007, T-009. High alignment with INV-codesamples-* suite. Effectiveness: 1.0.

sources.name: Enhancing code clarity through named parameters in C#
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 3 (DOC-2847): Source consulted but no independent recommendations originated. Content overlapped with SRC-007 (Microsoft guide). Redundant source.

sources.name: Fluent Builder Pattern — wolfsys.net
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 3 (DOC-2847): REC-004 (minimal and maximal fluent chain examples) cited by T-001. Applicable to additionalQueryConfiguration lambda chains. Niche but directly useful.

sources.name: Naming guidelines for boolean variables — Serendipidata
sources.assessmentDetail: Run 3 (DOC-2847): REC-002 (property vs method disambiguation) cited by T-001. Directly addressed IncludeUrlPath vs UrlPathColumns() confusion. Niche but precisely targeted.

sources.name: Code sample accuracy verification / documentation code block linting strategies
sources.rationale: Run 3 exposed pre-existing non-existent API method calls in documentation code blocks (TopN, OrderByDescending). No existing source addresses doc-embedded code verification strategies. Priority: medium.
sources.assessmentDetail: Candidate from DOC-2847 context signal analysis. Would help future accuracy reviews catch phantom API calls in .md code blocks.

sources.name: Role-based access control (RBAC) documentation patterns
sources.rationale: DOC-3167 documented a multi-layer permission model (application → ACL → workflow) with non-obvious interaction rules. No consulted source addressed RBAC/permission documentation patterns specifically. A source covering permission matrix documentation, prerequisite-chain documentation, and bypass-rule documentation would have been directly applicable to T-001 (19 acceptance criteria, 3 layers to disambiguate). Priority: medium.
sources.assessmentDetail: Candidate from DOC-3167 context signal analysis. Would help future permission documentation tasks with multi-layer models.
